title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Jimmy McGregor informs Anthony Ked that he has received a mysterious package containing the memoirs of Count Stapfitch, a powerful former Prime Minister of Hesoslovakia. Jimmy recounts that four years ago in Paris, he rescued the Count from a group of thugs. The Count had settled in Paris following the assassination of King Nikolai IV, whose reign ended in a republic after a popular uprising against his marriage to a commoner, Empress Farajia.

Two weeks ago, Jimmy received the manuscript along with a letter promising a £1,000 reward if the memoirs are delivered to a London publisher by October 13th. Because Jimmy has already booked a trip to Cape Town to travel through the African interior, he is unable to complete the delivery himself. Anthony agrees to take the job for a quarter of the reward, amounting to £250. The two men agree that Anthony will travel on the ship *Granada* using Jimmy's name as an alias.

As they finalize their plans, Anthony expresses skepticism regarding the logistics of the mission, questioning why the Count's estate would send a manuscript from France through Africa to England rather than mailing it directly. Anthony muses that the publication of such biographies often causes trouble, suggesting that the deceased Count may have intended to stir up controversy from beyond the grave.
